summary: @torbeck/heap ships an obfuscated dropper that fetches and executes remote code on require()
details: The package is a typosquat of @datastructures-js/heap: package.json declares author 'Eyas Ranjous <eyas-ranjous@gmail.com>', repository 'git+https://github.com/datastructures-js/heap.git', and homepage on github.com/datastructures-js/heap, while publishing under the unrelated @torbeck scope. The Heap/MinHeap/MaxHeap API in src/heap.js is byte-identical to the legitimate package, providing cover for a ~95KB obfuscator.io payload appended after `exports.Heap = Heap;` at src/heap.js:425. The payload uses a rotated 580-entry string array with an RC4-based decoder (function a5), a second 612-entry array, control-flow flattening, and an anti-debug FSM. When the module is require()'d, the IIFE checks for Linux non-container hosts and absence of -e/-p/-c node argv flags, sets a marker env var to prevent re-entry, performs an HTTPS GET (hostname/path reconstructed from decoded strings, up to 5 redirects), writes the response to a platform-tagged filename in os.tmpdir(), AES-256-GCM-decrypts it, then executes it via `spawn('sh', ['-c',...], {stdio:[ignore,ignore,ignore], timeout:2000})` and a detached respawn of node with the dropped file as argv0. A.lock file and sidecar.json are written for persistence and to suppress re-entry. Any installer who require()s this package on a Linux build/CI host immediately runs attacker-controlled code with the developer's privileges.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
